documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)

cards.documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)
cards.source: Fictional QC OAS Application, p. 1, par. 1
cards.text: The Applicant is a public health institution operating psychiatric services in Montreal.

cards.documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)
cards.source: Fictional QC OAS Application, p. 1, par. 2
cards.text: The Respondent is 45 years old, lives alone, and is currently hospitalized at the Applicant institution since May 28, 2026.

cards.documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)
cards.source: Fictional QC OAS Application, p. 1, par. 3
cards.text: The Respondent has advanced education and presents as articulate; however, the Applicant submits that articulation has repeatedly co-existed with clinical destabilization.

cards.documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)
cards.source: Fictional QC OAS Application, p. 1, par. 4
cards.text: The Respondent is presently under a detention order following psychiatric evaluations; copies are referenced herein but not appended in this Application package.

cards.documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)
cards.source: Fictional QC OAS Application, p. 1, par. 5
cards.text: Following assessment, the treating psychiatrist concludes that the Respondent suffers from a chronic psychotic-spectrum disorder with episodic mood elevation and impaired insight, as appears from the medical report communicated as Exhibit P-1.

cards.documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)
cards.source: Fictional QC OAS Application, p. 1, par. 6
cards.text: The Respondent rejects the diagnosis and describes it as a narrative imposed by institutions.

cards.documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)
cards.source: Fictional QC OAS Application, p. 2, par. 7
cards.text: The present Application is the fourth request for authorization of care concerning the Respondent.

cards.documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)
cards.source: Fictional QC OAS Application, p. 2, par. 8
cards.text: A first authorization was granted in 2006 for three years; a second in 2010 for two years; and a third in 2021 for three years (references in file).

cards.documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)
cards.source: Fictional QC OAS Application, p. 2, par. 9
cards.text: The Applicant relies on the fact that stabilization occurred during prior court-supervised periods, while discontinuation occurred shortly after expiry of supervision.

cards.documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)
cards.source: Fictional QC OAS Application, p. 2, par. 10
cards.text: The Respondent states that past orders were administrative mistakes and denies that medication contributed to stability.

cards.documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)
cards.source: Fictional QC OAS Application, p. 2, par. 11
cards.text: The Respondent has been known to psychiatric services since 2001. The file contains multiple admissions between 2001 and 2010, then again in 2018, 2021 and 2026.

cards.documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)
cards.source: Fictional QC OAS Application, p. 2, par. 12
cards.text: During periods of destabilization, the file refers to: distrust toward authority, escalating complaints, grandiose certainty, and categorical refusal of treatment.

cards.documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)
cards.source: Fictional QC OAS Application, p. 2, par. 13
cards.text: The Applicant notes that the Respondent's current insistence on procedural legitimacy resembles the tone documented in 2006 and 2010, even if the Respondent presents it as reasoned legal critique.

cards.documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)
cards.source: Fictional QC OAS Application, p. 2, par. 14
cards.text: In 2006, notes reference persistent oppositional discourse in relation to treatment. The Respondent recalls that admission as voluntary and disputes the interpretation.

cards.documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)
cards.source: Fictional QC OAS Application, p. 2, par. 15
cards.text: In late 2025, the Respondent began to raise repeated concerns about institutional record-keeping, fairness of administrative processes, and the legitimacy of psychiatric conclusions.

cards.documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)
cards.source: Fictional QC OAS Application, p. 2, par. 16
cards.text: The Respondent states these concerns are grounded in specific events; the Applicant submits they must be interpreted in light of longstanding themes of institutional mistrust documented over years.

cards.documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)
cards.source: Fictional QC OAS Application, p. 2, par. 17
cards.text: On May 12, 2026, outpatient notes describe the Respondent as increasingly fixed on procedural integrity, repeatedly requesting formal written proof and refusing to proceed unless such proof is provided.

cards.documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)
cards.source: Fictional QC OAS Application, p. 2, par. 18
cards.text: On May 28, 2026, the Respondent was brought to hospital following concerns reported by third parties regarding deterioration in functioning. Those third-party reports are summarized in Exhibit P-1 but are not attached as sworn statements.

cards.documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)
cards.source: Fictional QC OAS Application, p. 2, par. 19
cards.text: Nursing notes indicate the Respondent was calm and cooperative at intake; later notes describe increasing insistence on debating the legal basis of care rather than discussing treatment.

cards.documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)
cards.source: Fictional QC OAS Application, p. 2, par. 20
cards.text: The Respondent denies hallucinations and denies risk; the Applicant submits that similar denials were recorded prior to earlier relapses.

cards.documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)
cards.source: Fictional QC OAS Application, p. 2, par. 21
cards.text: The Respondent has remained able to comply with ward routine; however, the Applicant submits that this does not exclude relapse and has not excluded relapse historically.

cards.documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)
cards.source: Fictional QC OAS Application, p. 2, par. 22
cards.text: The Respondent refuses pharmacological treatment. The refusal is presented as rational and rights-based.

cards.documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)
cards.source: Fictional QC OAS Application, p. 2, par. 23
cards.text: The Applicant submits that refusal in this context is itself a clinical feature reflecting absent insight, as per Exhibit P-1.

cards.documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)
cards.source: Fictional QC OAS Application, p. 3, par. 24
cards.text: The Respondent states willingness to engage in non-pharmacological support, but rejects medication categorically.

cards.documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)
cards.source: Fictional QC OAS Application, p. 3, par. 25
cards.text: The Applicant submits that without treatment the Respondent's condition will deteriorate further, with increased likelihood of conflict, functional decline, and recurrent hospitalization.

cards.documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)
cards.source: Fictional QC OAS Application, p. 3, par. 26
cards.text: The Applicant acknowledges that immediate danger is not documented in the present file. The Applicant nevertheless submits that deterioration is foreseeable based on the longitudinal record.

cards.documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)
cards.source: Fictional QC OAS Application, p. 3, par. 27
cards.text: The Applicant asserts that the benefits of treatment outweigh the risks, and that side effects can be managed by adjustment, discontinuation, or adjunct medication, as per Exhibit P-1.

cards.documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)
cards.source: Fictional QC OAS Application, p. 3, par. 28
cards.text: The Applicant seeks authorization to administer antipsychotic medication, oral or intramuscular, alone or in combination, at the lowest effective dose, as determined by treating physicians.

cards.documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)
cards.source: Fictional QC OAS Application, p. 3, par. 29
cards.text: The Applicant seeks authorization to administer mood stabilizers and other adjunct medication as deemed clinically appropriate.

cards.documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)
cards.source: Fictional QC OAS Application, p. 3, par. 30
cards.text: The Applicant seeks authorization to conduct blood tests, laboratory examinations, ECG and other monitoring as required by protocols.

cards.documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)
cards.source: Fictional QC OAS Application, p. 3, par. 31
cards.text: The Applicant seeks authorization to hospitalize the Respondent until the treating physician judges stabilization sufficient for safe discharge; and to re-hospitalize in the event of deterioration for a period not exceeding thirty days per episode.

cards.documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)
cards.source: Fictional QC OAS Application, p. 3, par. 32
cards.text: Authorization is sought for a period of three years, described as the minimal time necessary to stabilize the Respondent and to potentially allow development of insight, as asserted in Exhibit P-1.

cards.documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)
cards.source: Fictional QC OAS Application, p. 3, par. 33
cards.text: It is required that the Applicant may call upon peace officers and paramedics to assist in execution of these authorizations, wherever the Respondent may be located.

cards.documentTitle: Introductory Application for Authorization of Care and Treatment (Fictional Jordan L.)
cards.source: Fictional QC OAS Application, p. 3, par. 34
cards.text: The Applicant requests that such assistance may be requested verbally, failing which the judgment risks remaining without effect.
